ocr.text: In the study of somite morphology, the identification of U-shaped somites is crucial for understanding developmental anomalies
in vertebrate embryos. The data presented in Table 1 encapsulates key metrics related to the frequency and characteristics of this
phenotype category, highlighting the prevalence of U-shaped somites among various embryonic candidates. Specifically, the
table indicates that there are eight candidates exhibiting this distinctive phenotype, all of which were subjected to a standardized
staining procedure utilizing Tris-buffered saline (TBS). This consistent application of staining across all samples ensures

reliability in visualizing the somite structures, thereby facilitating accurate comparison and analysis.

Moreover, the reported
frequency of U-shaped somites stands at 114 occurrences, reflecting its significant presence within the sampled population.
Phenotype category  Description  Number Candidatesa
U-shaped Somites  Somites with characteristic U shape  8
Cyclopia  Single large eye  6
Craniofacial abnormalities  Small/missing mandible; skull abnormalities  4
Pigment abnormalities  Floating melanocytes; reduced pigment in eye  3
Heart  Pericardial edema  1
Other  Tail/fin abnormalities, embryo lethality  2
Stains  Procedure site  Frequency  Percent  Staining time Minutes  Report issuing site
TBS  Onsite  114  100.0  3.00  On site in Procedure room
Not onsite  0  0.00
Pap  Onsite  0  0.00  30.00  In the Laboratory after 24 hours
Not onsite  114  100.0
Stains  Staining ability  Staining intensity  Permanent smearing  Cell membrane and cytoplasm  Nuclei detail
TBS  Good  Good  Good  Good  Good
PAP  Good  Good  Good  Good  Good
This finding is particularly noteworthy as it corresponds to a 100.0% representation, underscoring the uniformity of this
phenotype among the examined subjects. The meticulous recording of staining times, which averaged 3.00 minutes, provides
additional context regarding the efficiency of the procedural methodology employed. The report issuing site, critical for
ensuring the integrity and traceability of the results, further emphasizes the structured approach taken in this investigation.
Collectively, these metrics not only elucidate the prevalence of U-shaped somites but also highlight the rigor of the procedural
framework that supports these findings.
